_note: Illustrative payload — content transcribed from a user-supplied reference image of the Seiji Deploy workflow Confluence diagram. When the bot runs this template live, K1-K2 must re-verify each lane, node, chip, tag, and source against the live Confluence page before rendering. Never fabricate node names, tool names, or repo paths.
question: Explain the Seiji Deploy workflow in detail with the help of a diagram.
tldr: Seiji orchestrates three data-plane paths — SQL Server outbound over an SSH tunnel, SFTP inbound from external servers, and SFTP outbound from S3 — with private keys held in AWS Secrets Manager and public keys distributed via tenant config.

side.title: Customer SFTP Access to Abacus (S3 Transfer)

items.title: AWS Transfer Family / Abacus SFTP endpoint

items.text: Private keys stored securely in AWS Secrets Manager (accessed by services)
Public keys stored in tenant config / authorized_keys (used to authorize access)

confidence.verified: Lane structure, node names, and key-management pattern taken from a user-supplied reference image of the Seiji Confluence diagram.
confidence.not_verified: Live Confluence URL, page-last-modified date, exact repo paths, and any recent changes not reflected in the reference image. Re-verify against the live Confluence page before treating this as authoritative.

for_you.text: Self-service: open the Seiji Deploy Workflow Confluence page (Abacus NextGen 2.0 space) to see the live version of this diagram and any tenant-specific overrides in ng-deployment-config-files.

Recommendation: if you are adding a new tenant, confirm the public-key hand-off (customer public key → tenant config) and the AWS Transfer Family endpoint provisioning are both scheduled before enabling the SFTP flow.
